name: Ghost in the Machine
description: A pure hacking build that turns Night City's own infrastructure against its inhabitants. Eliminate entire buildings of enemies without ever firing a shot. Upload quickhacks from stealth, spread contagions through networks, and watch chaos unfold from the safety of a camera feed.
key_perks: Bartmoss' Legacy - Craft legendary quickhacks
Plague - Quickhacks spread to additional targets
Subliminal Message - Quickhacks deal more damage to unaware enemies
Critical Error - Quickhacks can deal critical hits
Queue Mastery - Reduced RAM cost for queued quickhacks
Hack Queue - Upload multiple quickhacks to a single target
Counter-a-hack - Prevents enemy netrunners from hacking you
Diffusion - Quickhack spread range increased
pros: Can clear entire encounters without being detected
Extremely powerful against groups of networked enemies
Minimal ammo and resource consumption
Legendary Short Circuit passive triggers on any critical hit
Trivializes many boss fights with Ultimate quickhacks
cons: Weak in direct combat if quickhacks are on cooldown
RAM management is crucial and mistakes are punishing
Slow start - build doesn't come online until mid-game
Vulnerable to enemies not connected to networks
Some boss encounters have quickhack resistance

name: Chrome Demon
description: A melee-focused powerhouse that dances through combat with katanas and Sandevistan. Slow time, close the gap, and carve through enemies like a cybernetic blender. Reflexes and Cool combine to create a lightning-fast killing machine that thrives in close quarters.
key_perks: Sting Like a Bee - Increased attack speed with blades
Roaring Waters - First strong attack deals massive bonus damage
Judge Jury Executioner - Increased damage against full-health enemies
Dragon Strike - Increased critical damage with katanas
Deathbolt - Kill restores health and increases movement speed
Fiery Blast - Strong attacks deal area damage
Cold Blood stacking perks for damage and speed
Frozen Precision - Increased headshot/critical damage with Cold Blood
pros: Incredibly fast and satisfying gameplay
Sandevistan makes you nearly untouchable
Massive single-target damage with katanas
Cold Blood stacks provide huge scaling
Can deflect bullets with katanas while approaching
cons: No hacking capability - Sandevistan replaces cyberdeck
Vulnerable during Sandevistan cooldown
Ranged enemies in open areas can be challenging
Requires good spatial awareness and positioning
Less effective against bosses with grab attacks

name: One-Person Army
description: A balanced combat build that excels with all weapon types. High Body and Reflexes create a versatile fighter who can switch between assault rifles, pistols, and shotguns seamlessly. Tough enough to face-tank damage and skilled enough to land every shot.
key_perks: Annihilation perks for shotgun/LMG damage
Speed Demon - Increased damage while moving
Mow Down - Increased LMG damage
Hail of Bullets - Reduced bullet spread while moving
Assault rifle and handgun damage perks
Steel Shell - Increased Armor
Cardio Cure - Health regen during combat
Epimorphosis - Faster health regeneration out of combat
pros: Very beginner-friendly and straightforward
Extremely tanky with high health and armor
Versatile across all combat scenarios
Berserk provides massive damage and survivability boost
Can brute force through any encounter
Strong from early game to endgame
cons: No hacking capability with Berserk equipped
Less elegant than specialized builds
Ammo consumption can be high
Limited stealth capability
Some situations favor precision over brute force

name: Shadow Operative
description: A Cold-blooded killer who eliminates targets without ever being seen. Combines silenced weapons, stealth movement, and precise headshots to clear encounters like a ghost. Patience is rewarded with devastating opening strikes and flawless escapes.
key_perks: Assassin - Deal 15% more damage to human enemies
From the Shadows - Crit chance increased when entering combat
Silent and Deadly - Increased damage from silenced weapons in stealth
Aggressive Antitoxins - Immunity to poison
Neurotoxin - All Cold Blood attacks apply poison
Merciless - Cold Blood stacks increase crit chance and damage
Ninjutsu - Sprinting does not break stealth
Hasty Retreat - Increased movement speed for 5 seconds after being detected
pros: Extremely satisfying when executed perfectly
One-shot kills on most enemies from stealth
Cold Blood stacks make you progressively deadlier
Can bypass many encounters entirely
Minimal resource usage per encounter
cons: Punishing if detected - limited combat survivability
Slower paced gameplay requiring patience
Some encounters force open combat
Boss fights require different approach
Requires good knowledge of enemy patrol patterns

name: Railgun Surgeon
description: A precision marksman who uses tech weapons to eliminate targets through walls and cover. Charged shots from tech sniper rifles penetrate any surface, turning the entire map into a shooting gallery. If you can see them on the scanner, you can kill them.
key_perks: Up to 11 - Allows tech weapons to charge to 100%
Tesla - Fully charged tech weapon shots deal 30% more damage
Fuck All Walls - Reduces charge amount needed for tech weapons to penetrate walls
Lickety Split - Tech weapons charge faster
Bladerunner - Increased damage to drones, mechs, and robots
Chain Lightning - Charged tech shots hit additional enemies
Revamp - Fully charged tech weapons deal double damage
Edgerunner Artisan - Craft legendary tier items
pros: Can kill enemies through walls and cover
Massive single-shot damage with charged weapons
Comrade's Hammer can one-shot nearly anything
Crafting perks provide the best gear in the game
Excellent at all ranges with different tech weapons
cons: Charge time leaves you vulnerable
Less effective in chaotic close-quarters combat
Relies heavily on Ping to see targets through walls
Tech ammo can be scarce early game
Positioning is critical for wall-penetration angles

name: Time Bandit
description: A speed-obsessed build that maximizes Sandevistan uptime and time-dilation combat. With heavily modded Sandevistan and cooldown reduction, you spend more time in slow-motion than real-time. Dash between enemies at impossible speed delivering critical hits to everything.
key_perks: Blinding Speed - Increases Sandevistan damage bonus
Deathbolt - Kills during Sandevistan restore health and movement speed
Cold Blood stacking for speed and damage
Rapid Bloodflow - Health regen with Cold Blood active
Sting Like a Bee - Attack speed with blades
Fiery Blast - AoE damage on strong melee attacks
Handgun and blade damage perks for versatility
Frozen Precision - Critical damage boost with Cold Blood
recommended_cyberware: QianT Sandevistan Mk.4 (modded for cooldown reduction)
pros: Sandevistan uptime of ~75% with proper modding
Incredibly fast-paced and thrilling gameplay
Versatile - effective with both melee and ranged
Cold Blood stacks amplify already high speed
Can dodge nearly all incoming damage during slow-time
cons: No hacking capability whatsoever
Still vulnerable during short cooldown windows
Requires investment in cooldown reduction cyberware
Less effective in stealth-focused scenarios
Can feel overwhelming to manage at high speed

name: Unstoppable Juggernaut
description: An unkillable wall of chrome and muscle that walks into gunfire and laughs. Maximum Body investment combined with Berserk operating system creates a bruiser who heals through damage and crushes everything with Gorilla Arms and shotguns. Why dodge when you can just absorb?
key_perks: Steel Shell - Massively increased Armor
Cardio Cure - Health regen in combat
Dog of War - Increased Berserk damage and duration
Unbreakable - Increased max health
Flattened - Enemies hit by strong attacks are knocked down
Crusher - Increased reload speed and damage with shotguns
Momentum Shift - Defeating enemy increases movement speed
Epimorphosis - Rapid out-of-combat healing
pros: Nearly impossible to kill with all survivability cyberware
Second Heart provides literal extra life
Gorilla Arms open all locked doors (Body checks)
Simple and satisfying smash-everything gameplay
Berserk makes you even tougher and stronger
Great for Very Hard difficulty
cons: No hacking capability
Slow gameplay compared to Sandevistan builds
Limited range without switching to guns
Some encounters with snipers require closing distance
Not suited for stealth at all

name: Digital Ronin
description: The best of both worlds - a build that combines Netrunner hacking with katana swordplay. Use quickhacks to soften groups, disable key targets, and debuff enemies before closing in with a blade. Intelligence and Reflexes create a versatile warrior-hacker hybrid.
key_perks: Plague - Quickhack spread for softening groups
Critical Error - Quickhacks can crit
Sting Like a Bee - Blade attack speed
Dragon Strike - Katana critical damage
Cold Blood stacking perks
Merciless - Crit chance and damage with Cold Blood
Subliminal Message - Extra quickhack damage on unaware enemies
Deathbolt - Health restore on kills
recommended_cyberware: Raven Microcyber Mk.4 Cyberdeck (balanced RAM and slots)
pros: Most versatile build in the game
Can handle any situation with hacking or melee
Weaken enemies with hacks then finish with blade
Never feels one-dimensional
Excellent in both stealth and open combat
cons: Jack of all trades, master of neither
Attribute spread means no single stat at 20 early
RAM management while also managing melee positioning is complex
Lower survivability than dedicated tank or netrunner
Requires good game knowledge to play optimally

name: Blade Dancer
description: A unique Cool-focused build that weaponizes throwing knives as the primary damage source. With the right perks, thrown knives return to you and deal devastating damage. Combined with stealth bonuses and Cold Blood, each throw is a potential one-hit kill. Elegant and deadly.
key_perks: Juggler - Thrown knives return to you after hitting
Dagger Dealer - Increased thrown knife damage
From the Shadows - Entering combat increases crit chance
Silent and Deadly - Silenced weapon damage bonus in stealth
Assassin - Bonus damage to human enemies
Neurotoxin - Apply poison through Cold Blood
Merciless - Cold Blood crit stacking
Ninjutsu - Sprinting does not break stealth
pros: Extremely satisfying one-hit knife kills
Knives return after Juggler perk - infinite ammo
Silent kills maintain stealth perfectly
Cold Blood stacks make each subsequent kill deadlier
Very unique and underutilized playstyle
cons: Very weak until Juggler perk is obtained
Limited area damage capability
Heavily reliant on specific perk investment
Open combat against large groups is challenging
Boss fights require backup weapon strategy

name: Relic Override
description: A Phantom Liberty build that fully exploits the new Relic skill tree. Combines the powerful new Relic perks with existing combat skills to create a cutting-edge build. Jailbreak abilities enhance arm cyberware, while Vulnerability Analytics turns every enemy into a weak point puzzle.
key_perks: Jailbreak - Unlocks new abilities for arm cyberware (Mantis Blades leap, Gorilla Arms shockwave, Monowire control, Launcher smart targeting)
Vulnerability Analytics - Enemies display weak points for bonus damage
Machine Learning - Hitting weak points increases crit stats temporarily
Limiter Removal - Overclock cyberware for increased power at health cost
Data Tunneling - Quickhacks applied through Monowire deal more damage
Launch Capacity Override - Projectile Launch System fires multiple projectiles
Sensory Protocol - Becoming harder to detect while crouching
Spatial Mapping - Increased weak point damage
recommended_cyberware: QianT Sandevistan Mk.4 or Arasaka Sandevistan
Mantis Blades (Legendary) - Enhanced by Jailbreak leap
recommended_weapons: Mantis Blades (Arm cyberware as primary weapon)
pros: Access to powerful Relic skill tree abilities
Jailbreak transforms arm cyberware into devastating weapons
Vulnerability Analytics provides consistent bonus damage
Machine Learning creates a snowball effect in combat
Most modern and updated build path in the game
cons: Requires Phantom Liberty DLC
Relic points are limited - must choose perks carefully
Limiter Removal health cost can be dangerous
Requires understanding of weak point mechanics
Build doesn't fully come online until late game with Relic points
